KPI Bluebook

Key Performance Indicators:

  • kpi flare recovery pct
  • SOx Emissions Concentration (kg/m3
  • Propylene to Ethylene (P/E) Ratio
  • Cooling Water Delta-T (°C
  • Yield Percentage (%
  • Mean Time Between Failures (MTBF
  • On-Spec Rate (%
  • Polymer Melt Flow Index (MFI) Variance
  • Maintenance to Operating Cost Ratio
  • Daily Feedstock Throughput (tons/day
  • OEE %
  • Specific Catalyst Cost ($/ton
KPI Bluebook
  • Propylene to Ethylene (P/E) Ratio
  • View page source

Propylene to Ethylene (P/E) Ratio

\[\text{}\]
Open in: VS Code PyCharm Edit
Description
  • The Propylene to Ethylene (P/E) Ratio measures the relative production of propylene compared to ethylene in petrochemical processes
  • The ratio of propylene to ethylene production volumes
  • used to assess the balance of the product slate
Objective
  • The objective of this KPI is to optimize the production processes to enhance the yield of propylene relative to ethylene
  • thereby improving overall profitability
  • Align production strategy with market demand and margins by monitoring relative propylene versus ethylene output
Formula

The P/E Ratio is calculated by dividing the total tons of propylene produced by the total tons of ethylene produced.

$$ \mathit{Result} = \frac{\mathit{0\,0;\,Return\,Propylene\,Produced\,Tons}}{\mathit{Ethylene\,Produced\,Tons;\,\}\,\}}} $$
Used in KPI(s)
  • This KPI is utilized in performance assessments related to olefin production efficiency and profitability analysis
Input Measure
  • The input measures for this KPI are the total tons of propylene and ethylene produced during a specified time period
  • Total propylene produced and total ethylene produced in the measurement period
Unit of Measure The unit of measure for the P/E Ratio is a dimensionless ratio, representing the quantity of propylene produced per unit of ethylene.
Reporting Source Data for this KPI is sourced from production reports and operational data from petrochemical facilities.
Comments Integrating processes like olefin cracking or metathesis units can increase the overall propylene yield and thus the P/E ratio.

Code Context

1// KPI: Propylene to Ethylene (P/E) Ratio

public static class KpiDemo {

public static double ComputePERatio(double propyleneProducedTons, double ethyleneProducedTons) {

if (ethyleneProducedTons == 0) return 0.0; return propyleneProducedTons / ethyleneProducedTons;

}

}
